Feat/registry provider (#322)

* Add ArtifactsProvider singleton in fiab-core for plugin access to artifact functions

- Move MlModelOverview dataclass from forecastbox.api.artifacts.base into
  fiab_core.artifacts, where it belongs alongside the other core artifact types
- Add fiab_core.providers.ArtifactsProvider: a singleton with register_*/provider

  can call these without depending on the forecastbox package

  before load_plugins, followed by refresh_catalog (correct init order)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ArtifactsProvider: use CheckpointLookup instead of list_models

- Revert move of MlModelOverview to fiab-core; it stays in forecastbox
- Add CheckpointLookup = Mapping[CompositeArtifactId, MlModelCheckpoint] to
  fiab_core.artifacts as the correct type for plugin consumption
- Replace list_models provider with get_checkpoint_lookup, which returns the
  live catalog mapping (registered as lambda: ArtifactManager.catalog so
  callers always see the current value, not a snapshot)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Delay plugin loading until catalog refresh completes

- Add delayed_thread(future, fn, args) to ecpyutil: returns a Thread that
  waits for the given Future before calling fn(*args); logs a warning and
  proceeds if the future raised an exception
- submit_refresh_catalog() now returns the Future[None] from the executor
- submit_load_plugins() accepts an optional start_after: Future[None]; when
  provided it uses delayed_thread so plugins load only after the catalog is ready
- Entrypoint chains them: catalog_ready = submit_refresh_catalog(),
  then submit_load_plugins(start_after=catalog_ready)

Startup order is now: initialize_stores → register providers →
submit_refresh_catalog → submit_load_plugins (waits for catalog)

commit.message:
Fix MLX GPU crash, Slack token update, and MCP auto-start

- Add MLXInferenceGate to serialize all Metal GPU inference (embeddings,
  code analysis, chat). Prevents concurrent MLX operations that crash
  the Metal driver with EXC_BAD_ACCESS/SIGABRT.
- Fix performSelfRestart() to cancel MLX gate before exit(0), preventing
  race between Metal teardown and in-flight inference.
- Fix Slack token update: connectWithToken() now writes directly to
  Keychain via updateToken() instead of file-then-configure path that
  kept reading the old token from Keychain.
- Auto-start MCP server on app launch when isEnabled is persisted true.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize worktree paths in RepoRegistry to prevent cache poisoning

When a worktree at .agent-workspaces/workspace-XYZ was registered, it
overwrote the correct repo root in the urlToPath/hashToPath maps. This
caused getLocalPath(for:) to return worktree paths, which the scheduler
then passed as workingDirectory to chains.run — leading to double-nested
worktrees and chains targeting wrong directories.

Fix: registerRepo() and registerRepo(remoteURL:localPath:) now strip
.agent-workspaces/ suffixes before storing, ensuring the canonical repo
root is always used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize worktree paths in project path resolution

When chains.run fell back to resolvedLastWorkingDirectory, it could
return a .agent-workspaces/workspace-* path instead of the actual repo
root. This caused chains to target worktree directories and show
incorrect paths in the UI Project field.

Fix:
- Add AgentManager.normalizeRepoPath() that strips .agent-workspaces/
  suffixes to recover the parent repo root
- Apply normalization in the lastUsedWorkingDirectory setter (prevents
  persisting worktree paths)
- Apply normalization in resolvedLastWorkingDirectory getter (catches
  stale values from UserDefaults)
- Apply normalization in chains.run handler as belt-and-suspenders

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

commit.message:
Fix main thread deadlock: move terminal.run Process to background queue

TerminalToolsHandler.executeCommand() used withCheckedContinuation with
process.waitUntilExit() which blocked the calling thread. When MCP
dispatched tool calls on the main actor, this froze the entire UI.

Spindump confirmed: main thread stuck at waitUntilExit for 27+ minutes.

Fix: wrap Process execution in DispatchQueue.global(qos: .userInitiated)
so waitUntilExit never blocks the main thread.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feature/collision resolution (#11)

* feat(tests): add integration tests for organize and scan commands

- Implement integration tests for the organize command, covering various scenarios including file organization by type, dry-run mode, manifest generation, naming conventions, and error handling.
- Implement integration tests for the scan command, verifying functionality for scanning directories, nested structures, file type filtering, and error handling.
- Update jest configuration to include integration tests in the test match pattern.

fix(organize): enhance config loading mechanism

- Modify the organize command to check for a configuration file in the target directory if none is specified in the options.
- Introduce a helper method to find configuration files in the directory.

fix(scan): enhance config loading mechanism

- Modify the scan command to check for a configuration file in the target directory if none is specified in the options.
- Introduce a helper method to find configuration files in the directory.

fix(manifest): add backward compatibility for manifest structure

🏗️ Week 3-4: Architecture improvements & resilience patterns

ARCHITECTURE:
- Centralize magic numbers: create config/apiConstants.ts (11 constant groups)
- Extract TIMEOUTS, RATE_LIMITS, CACHE, SEARCH, AI_LIMITS, VALIDATION_LIMITS
- Replace 25+ hardcoded numbers with named constants

RESILIENCE PATTERNS:
- Implement Circuit Breaker pattern for external services
- FatSecret API now protected by circuit breaker (3 failures → 30s cooldown)
- Google Maps, Airtable, Gemini circuit breakers configured
- Circuit opens after threshold, auto-recovers via HALF_OPEN state

DATA INTEGRITY:
- Fix Pantry race conditions: replace setDoc with runTransaction
- Atomic pantry updates prevent concurrent edit conflicts
- Transaction ensures read-modify-write atomicity

FILES CHANGED:
+ src/config/apiConstants.ts (NEW - centralized configuration)
+ src/lib/api/utils/circuit-breaker.ts (NEW - resilience pattern)
+ src/utils/emojiUtils.ts (from Week 2)
~ src/pages/api/recommend.ts (use constants, cleaner code)
~ src/lib/api/utils/fatsecret.ts (circuit breaker integration)
~ src/hooks/usePantry.ts (transaction-based updates)

IMPACT:
- Easier maintenance: change timeout in 1 place, not 25
- Better resilience: external API failures don't cascade
- Data safety: no more lost pantry updates from race conditions
- Self-healing: circuit breaker auto-recovers when service stabilizes

PromptKit fixes that — composable, version-controlled prompt components (personas, protocols, formats, templates) that snap together into reliable, repeatable prompts for bug investigation, design docs, code review, security audits, and more. Works with any LLM.

commit.message:
Refresh git commit hash every ~5 minutes in heartbeat cycle

The swarm node commit hash was set once at startup and never updated,
causing stale ⚠️ 'old code' warnings in the UI after git pull/push.

- Make currentGitCommitHash() public for periodic refresh
- Add withGitCommitHash() copy helper to WorkerCapabilities
- Refresh hash every 30 heartbeats (~5 min) in currentWorkerStatus()
- Brain and peer nodes now report live git HEAD, not stale build hash

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix rag.repos.list missing embeddingModel/dims and add analyzed/enriched counts

- Pass embeddingModel and embeddingDimensions from RAGCore RepoInfo through
  to RAGToolRepoInfo (were silently dropped, defaulting to nil)
- Add per-repo analyzedCount and enrichedCount to repos list output so
  callers get a complete picture without needing separate enrich.status calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add checks_by_prep() and all_preps() for group-based check selection (#239)

* Add checks_by_prep() and all_preps() for group-based check selection

Two new exported functions:
- all_preps(): lists registered prep names
- checks_by_prep(prep): returns check names belonging to given prep(s)

This solves the confusion between prep names and check names by
letting users select checks by group:

  gp(".", checks = checks_by_prep(c("description", "lintr")))
  gp(".", checks = checks_by_prep(NULL))  # prep-free checks only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use ... instead of c() for checks_by_prep

checks_by_prep("description", "lintr") reads more naturally than
checks_by_prep(c("description", "lintr")). No-prep checks are
now selected with checks_by_prep() instead of checks_by_prep(NULL).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Return empty character not NULL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* lintr tab fix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix checks_by_prep() returning NULL instead of character(0)

names(Filter(...)) returns NULL on empty results, which is dangerous
because gp(checks = NULL) means "run all checks". Also fixes
describe_check() indentation and strengthens the unknown-prep test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unreachable NULL guards in checks_by_prep()

Filter() always returns a named list, so names() returns character(0)
not NULL. The guards were untestable dead code causing a coverage gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename prep→group in user-facing API and assign groups to all checks

Addresses mpadge's review: users shouldn't need to understand "preps"
to select checks by category.

- Rename all_preps() → all_check_groups()
- Rename checks_by_prep() → checks_by_group()
- Add no-op "source" prep for 9 checks that inspect files directly
- Every check now belongs to at least one group
- Add structural validation tests (all checks grouped, groups map to
  preps, expect_false exclusion assertions)
- Update vignette to use "check group" terminology

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve check group UX: split source, unify tidyverse, rename options

- Split "source" into "code_structure" (treesitter checks) and
  "package_structure" (file existence checks)
- Unify all tidyverse checks under one "tidyverse" group (renamed
  from "tidyverse_lintr"), including file naming and export order
- Rename option/envvar: exclude_preps → exclude_check_groups,
  GP_EXCLUDE_PREPS → GP_EXCLUDE_CHECK_GROUPS
- Add warning when checks_by_group() receives unknown group names
- Purge all "prep" terminology from user-facing docs and API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename available_packages group to revdep

User-facing group name should describe what it does, not the
internal mechanism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename state$available_packages to state$revdep for consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add clarifying comment

Co-authored-by: mark padgham <mark.padgham@email.com>

* add clearer example

Co-authored-by: mark padgham <mark.padgham@email.com>

* Regenerate man pages after accepting mpadge's example suggestions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add NEWS entries for check group API, roxygen2, and duplicate checks

  current ALS file, skipping HEAD blob download on the fast path
- Add generateCommitMessage() export: ports buildCommitMessage() logic
  from JS to Rust, removing the last JS function that read DiffReport data
- Convert all NAPI exports to async via tokio::task::spawn_blocking,
  keeping the Electron main process unblocked during ALS parsing

  backwards-compatible snapshot deserialization

  based XML parsing, decompression, structuralCompareAls, buildLocalDiffFromAls,
  getAlsContent, and related helpers; retain only gitBin + git shell commands
- Replace get-als-content and no-commits fallback handlers with parseAls
- Remove buildCommitMessage() from main.ts
- Delete 9 orphaned types from types/index.ts (DecompressedAls, AlsChange,
  StructuralCompareResult, AudioTrackEntry, MidiTrackEntry, etc.)
- Uninstall fast-xml-parser (removes 68 packages)

JS is now purely transport: paths and buffers only. All ALS semantics
live in the Rust crate. One parser to update if Ableton's format changes.

Add no-console ESLint rule (#583)

Adds `no-console` rule to prevent accidental debugging statements in
production code.

## Changes

- Added `"no-console": "error"` to `.eslintrc.json` rules section

The rule enforces that console methods (log, warn, error, etc.) cannot
be used in linted source files. Build scripts and test configurations
remain unaffected as they fall outside the linting scope.

```json
"rules": {
  "react/react-in-jsx-scope": "off",
  "@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports": "error",
  "no-console": "error"
}
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Move UI concerns out of library

- Remove FORMAT_OPTIONS, memory helpers (getDefaultMemoryLimitMB,
  getDeviceMaxMemoryMB, MEMORY_STEP, MEMORY_MIN_MB) — these are
  consumer-side UI configuration
- Remove extractLabel and toGeoJSON from ExtentData — presentation
  logic belongs in the caller
- ExtentData.fetchExtents now returns raw nested data:
  rgExtents is { filename: { rg_N: bbox } } instead of flattened
- MetadataProvider.getParquetUrl is now identity (no .pmtiles/.mosaic.json
  assumption) — consumers subclass for custom URL schemes
- DefaultMetadataProvider.getMetaJsonUrl derives from getParquetUrl()
- Add WIP notice to README
